entry.title: BIP 118 and SIGHASH_ANYPREVOUT
entry.summary:
The recent updates to BIP 118 reflect a significant evolution in its development, primarily focusing on the incorporation of the ANYPREVOUT feature. After extensive discussions, it was decided that updating the existing BIP 118 would be more beneficial than assigning a new BIP number. This decision led to the creation of a draft PR on GitHub, which is accessible for review and discussion at [this link](https://github.com/bitcoin/bips/pull/943). Further details and the rationale behind the changes can also be explored in the updated BIP text available [here](https://github.com/ajtowns/bips/blob/bip-anyprevout/bip-0118.mediawiki).
One of the notable changes in the revised BIP 118 is the removal of chaperone signatures. This adjustment implies that while these signatures are no longer inherently part of the proposal, they can still be implemented by users if needed. Additionally, the update includes a modification to the ANYPREVOUTANYSCRIPT component, which now does not commit to the transaction value. This change aims to enhance flexibility and adaptability within the protocol.
These updates have not yet been accompanied by tested code, highlighting an opportunity for further contribution from the community to discuss and refine the proposal. The openness of the drafting process invites feedback and collaborative development, ensuring that the final implementation aligns closely with user needs and technological advancements in the cryptocurrency domain.
